steps.title: What ABC precomputes once at startup
steps.narrative: Extra_Truth4VarNPN (in extraUtilMisc.c:643) builds four parallel arrays indexed by 16-bit truth tables (65,536 entries each). For every 4-input function it records its NPN class index and the canonicalizing permutation+phase. The library is built once when Dec_ManStart runs (called by rewrite, refactor, etc.).

panes.title: Lookup arrays
rows: low 4 bits = input inverters; bit 4 = output negation
canonical TT (lex-smallest representative of the class)

panes.body: 222 classes partition the 65,536 functions. The asserts at extraUtilMisc.c:716 and decMan.c:716 confirm this count.

steps.title: How the table is built (sketch)
steps.narrative:
For each truth table u in [1, 32768) not yet assigned a class:
  1. Allocate a new class index.
  2. For each of the 16 input-phase masks i and 24 permutations k:
    • Compute uPerm = perm(polarize(u, i), k).
    • Mark uPerm and its complement as members of this class.
This explores all 32 × 24 = 768 NPN-equivalent forms. Note: NPN's "N" counts both input and output negation; the inner loop also processes ~u.

steps.title: Lookup example: aoi3's f-function
steps.narrative: Take the natural cut {a, b, c, d} at f. The function is f = ab + ad + cd. Its 16-bit truth table u, written MSB-first by input combination (d,c,b,a):

steps.title: Three array reads → class + canonical form
steps.narrative: rewrite reads pMap[0xFA88], pPhases[0xFA88], pPerms[0xFA88]. The phase mask says which inputs to invert (and whether to invert the output); the perm index selects one of 24 reorderings of (x0,x1,x2,x3). Apply both to the cut's fanins to bring them into canonical order — then the cut's truth table matches the class's canonical representative bit-for-bit.

entries.pPhases[u]: 5-bit value; low 4 bits = input inverters, bit 4 = output negation
entries.puCanons[u]: the canonical TT for this class

panes.body: (Exact values depend on the build's tie-breaking; rewrite's trace prints the resulting canonical TT and class index as TT and Class.)

steps.title: Why rewrite uses this
steps.narrative:
The library's per-class subgraph list (p->vClasses[k]) stores AIG implementations in canonical-form coordinate space. To re-use a stored subgraph for our cut, we must:
  1. permute fanins by pPerms[u],
  2. complement them by the low 4 bits of pPhases[u],
  3. and complement the output by bit 4 of pPhases[u].
That maps any of the 768 NPN-equivalent 4-input cuts to the same precomputed implementations.

panes.body: Same trick is used by map (supergate library) and by exact-synthesis caches.
